name: Zion Suzuki
reason: Japan's confirmed No.1 GK at the 2026 World Cup; started the group stage. The roster previously lacked him, so the auto-selected starter wrongly fell to Hayakawa/Osako.

name: Ayumu Seko
reason: Started in Japan's back three (3-4-2-1) at the 2026 World Cup (e.g. vs Sweden: Seko, Itakura, H. Ito). Was missing from the roster, so a winger was wrongly slotted at the 3rd centre-back.
source: World Soccer Talk / beIN Sports confirmed 2026 WC group-stage lineups

name: Manuel Neuer
reason: Germany's confirmed No.1 GK at the 2026 World Cup (came out of international retirement); started the group stage ahead of Baumann.

name: Gregor Kobel
reason: Switzerland's confirmed No.1 GK at the 2026 World Cup after Yann Sommer's international retirement; started the group stage.

name: Max Crocombe
reason: New Zealand's confirmed No.1 GK at the 2026 World Cup; won the starting battle and started the group stage.

name: Unai Simon
reason: Spain's confirmed No.1 GK at the 2026 World Cup; De la Fuente started Simon over Raya and Joan Garcia in the group stage.

name: Keito Nakamura
reason: Confirmed Japan starter at the 2026 World Cup (scored Japan's first goal/equalizer vs Netherlands and supplied the cross for Kamada's goal vs Tunisia). He was entirely missing from the roster, so the left shadow-striker slot was wrongly auto-filled.
source: EA SPORTS FC 26 official ratings + FOX Sports 2026 World Cup match reports

name: Antonio Nusa
reason: Confirmed Norway starter at the 2026 WC: started the R32 win vs Ivory Coast on the left wing. The roster lacked a natural left winger, so the slot was wrongly auto-filled by a striker (Sorloth).
source: World Soccer Talk — Ivory Coast vs Norway confirmed lineups (2026 WC R32)

name: Joel Ordonez
reason: Confirmed Ecuador starter at the 2026 WC: started the R32 vs Mexico in the back three (Ordonez-Pacho-Hincapie). Felix Torres, previously assumed, was a substitute. The roster's 3rd centre-back was missing so the slot was auto-filled out of position.
source: khelnow / FanDuel Research — Mexico vs Ecuador confirmed lineups (2026 WC R32)

name: Ivan Perisic
reason: Confirmed Croatia left wing-back at the 2026 WC (started LWB vs England, LB vs Ghana). Roster listed him only as LW so the LB/wing-back slot was wrongly auto-filled by a striker (Kramaric).

name: Maximiliano Araujo
reason: Uruguay's left-sided attacker in Bielsa's front three (Canobbio-Nunez-Araujo) at the 2026 WC. The roster had no natural left winger so the slot was auto-filled by a central midfielder (Bentancur).
source: FourFourTwo / SI / Al Jazeera — Uruguay confirmed & predicted 2026 WC lineups

name: Ben Doak
reason: Confirmed Scotland starter at the 2026 WC: started on the right wing vs Brazil (Group C) and created several chances vs Haiti. The roster lacked a natural right winger, so the slot was wrongly auto-filled by a left-back (Kieran Tierney).
source: ESPN — Scotland vs Brazil confirmed line-ups (2026 WC Group C)

name: Hakan Calhanoglu
reason: Turkiye's captain and chief playmaker (regista at Inter); confirmed starter at the 2026 World Cup. The roster previously lacked him entirely, so the auto-selected midfield wrongly omitted the team's key player.
source: Olympics.com FIFA 2026 WC squad guide + Transfermarkt profile
